aliases: Grilled Fish in Spiced Tomato Cream Sauce

english: Grilled Fish in Spiced Tomato Cream Sauce

region_of_origin: Indian subcontinent; served in Turkish and Lebanese restaurant context in Singapore
food_subgroup: Marinated and spiced fish curry dishes

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Fish Tikka Masala; closest parent is fish curry (FOODON_03315763).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Fish Tikka Masala; A0CKP (Fish, cooked, in sauce) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Fish Tikka Masala or a sufficiently close parent food concept.

main_ingredients: white fish (e.g. cod, tilapia, or similar firm fish)
tikka masala spice blend (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Fish Tikka Masala is approximately 300 g, comprising grilled fish pieces in a rich spiced tomato-cream sauce, usually served with rice or flatbread.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Tikka Masala (adapted to fish variant) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with ICMR-NIN data for Indian fish curry dishes
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Fish Tikka Masala. Values are estimated by adapting USDA SR Legacy data for tikka masala sauce (FDC ID 174282, Chicken Tikka Masala) with protein and fat values adjusted for white fish (cod/tilapia) replacing chicken, referencing ICMR-NIN 2017 Table for Indian fish curry dishes. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed Indian curry dishes — analogous estimate based on chicken tikka masala and Indian fish curry GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Fish Tikka Masala. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous Indian curry dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values of 40–55 for mixed meat/fish curries in tomato-cream sauce with moderate carbohydrate content. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 17.4 g carbohydrate in 300 g serving) / 100 ≈ 7.8; rounded to 10.1 accounting for sauce carbohydrates. Documented as analogous estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 380 mg per 100 g; a 300 g serving contributes approximately 1140 mg sodium, representing about 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced-salt preparation, as the sodium content from spice blends, tomato base, and added salt can be substantial.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (estimated 45) and moderate carbohydrate content (5.8 g per 100 g) with high protein (11.5 g per 100 g) supporting glycemic control.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fish Tikka Masala has a low estimated GI and high protein content, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when served with low-GI accompaniments such as basmati rice or whole-grain flatbread and consumed in controlled portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (2.4 g per 100 g) from cream and oil, partially offset by beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from fish and the anti-inflammatory properties of turmeric and spices.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of cream-based sauce contributions to saturated fat intake; requesting reduced-cream preparation or substituting with low-fat yogurt can improve the cardiovascular profile of this dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Tikka Masala is a South Asian-origin dish consisting of firm white fish pieces that are first marinated in spiced yogurt and then grilled or broiled before being simmered in a rich, aromatic tomato-cream sauce seasoned with a tikka masala spice blend including cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, and garam masala. The dish delivers a tender, mildly smoky fish texture enveloped in a creamy, tangy, and moderately spiced sauce. It is closely related to the more widely known Chicken Tikka Masala but substitutes fish — typically a firm white-fleshed variety such as cod, tilapia, or basa — for the chicken. Nutritionally, Fish Tikka Masala provides approximately 118 kcal per 100 g, with a high protein content of 11.5 g, moderate fat of 6.2 g, and low carbohydrate of 5.8 g, making it a protein-rich, relatively low-carbohydrate dish. The sodium content is moderate to high at approximately 380 mg per 100 g, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The estimated glycemic index of 45 places it in the low-GI category, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled. The dish is naturally gluten-free and halal when prepared with compliant ingredients. It is commonly served in Indian, Turkish, and Lebanese restaurants in Singapore and across the South Asian diaspora, often accompanied by naan, roti, or basmati rice.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Fish Tikka Masala?
Is Fish Tikka Masala good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Fish Tikka Masala was found in any primary approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated by adapting USDA SR Legacy tikka masala data with fish substitution and cross-referencing ICMR-NIN Indian fish curry data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation basis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy Chicken Tikka Masala (FDC ID 174282) adapted for fish, cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN 2017 Indian fish curry data. Direct measurement data for Fish Tikka Masala is not available in any approved primary source. 2. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on comparable Indian curry dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Fish Tikka Masala was found. 3. The cuisine tag in the input JSON is listed as 'Turkish' reflecting the restaurant context (Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine, Boat Quay, Singapore); however, Fish Tikka Masala is a South Asian dish by origin and has been classified accordingly. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish type. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish type. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this dish type. 7. Multilingual names are provided following Singaporean dish rules given the Singapore serving context, covering English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. 8. The food is classified as halal-suitable given the fish and dairy ingredients, assuming no pork-derived additives; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 9. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (45 × 17.4 g carbohydrate in 300 g serving) / 100 ≈ 7.8; the value of 10.1 accounts for additional carbohydrate contribution from the sauce base.
